assert._comment: Measured against a local production build of the commit under test (see .github/workflows/lighthouse.yml), so a PR is graded on its own code. Accessibility (>=0.95) is the hard gate. Performance is intentionally 'warn', not 'error': Lighthouse performance is Mapbox-GL-runtime-bound (~0.47 mobile, throttled 4x CPU), so a hard 0.65 floor stayed permanently red and drowned out real regressions. As a warning the check is green when healthy and only reddens on an accessibility regression. The real fix — lazy-loading the map to raise the score — is tracked in #226; re-promote performance to 'error' at a verified floor once that lands. Lighthouse is not a required status check, so it never blocks merge regardless.
